comparison.description: Compare two JSON objects to identify differences in structure and values.
comparison.shortDescription: Find differences between two JSON objects
comparison.title: Compare JSON

escapeJson.description: Escape special characters in JSON strings. Convert JSON data to properly escaped format for safe transmission or storage.
escapeJson.title: Escape JSON

ui.description1: In this example, we escape all quotes (") around the keys and values in a simple JSON object. This ensures that the JSON data is interpreted correctly if it's used in another JSON object or assigned to a variable as a string.
ui.description2:
In this example, we escape a more complex JSON object with nested elements containing data about the Margherita pizza recipe. We escape all quotes within the object as well as convert all line breaks into special "
" characters. Additionally, we wrap the entire output in double quotes by enabling the "Wrap Output in Quotes" option.
ui.title3: Escape a JSON Array of Numbers
ui.description3: This example showcases that escaping isn't necessary for JSON arrays containing only numbers. Since numbers themselves don't hold special meaning in JSON, the tool doesn't modify the input and the output remains the same as the original JSON array.
ui.description4: Add double quotes around the output JSON data.

jsonToCsv.description: Convert JSON data to CSV format. Supports nested objects and arrays, automatic header detection, and configurable delimiters and quoting — ready for Excel, Google Sheets, or any spreadsheet application.

alwaysQuote.description: Wrap every cell in double-quotes, regardless of its content.
alwaysQuote.label: Always quote

autoQuote.description: Only quote cells that contain the delimiter, a double-quote, or a newline. Keeps output clean and minimal.
autoQuote.label: Auto (recommended)

options.delimiter: Character used to separate values in each row (e.g. comma, semicolon, tab)

header.description: Use JSON keys as column names in the first row. Recommended for readability and import compatibility.
header.label: Include header row

jsonToCsv.title: JSON to CSV

ui.description1: Convert multiple JSON objects into CSV rows, one row per object.
ui.description2: Nested keys are flattened using dot notation (e.g. address.city).
ui.description3: Missing keys are filled with empty values to keep columns aligned.

jsonToXml.description: Convert JSON data to XML format. Transform structured JSON objects into well-formed XML documents.
jsonToXml.title: JSON to XML

ui.description1: Convert a simple JSON object into an XML format.
ui.description2: Use spaces to visualize the hierarchical structure of XML.
ui.description3: Use tabs to visualize the hierarchical structure of XML.
ui.description5: Add a meta tag at the beginning of the XML output.

minify.description: Remove all unnecessary whitespace from JSON.
minify.title: Minify JSON

toolInfo.description: JSON minification is the process of removing all unnecessary whitespace characters from JSON data while maintaining its validity. This includes removing spaces, newlines, and indentation that aren't required for the JSON to be parsed correctly. Minification reduces the size of JSON data, making it more efficient for storage and transmission while keeping the exact same data structure and values.
toolInfo.title: What Is JSON Minification?

ui.description1: This example shows how to minify a simple JSON object by removing all unnecessary whitespace.
ui.description2: This example demonstrates minification of a complex nested JSON structure with arrays and objects.

prettify.description: Format JSON with proper indentation and spacing.
prettify.title: Prettify JSON

toolInfo.description: This tool allows you to format JSON data with proper indentation and spacing, making it more readable and easier to work with.
toolInfo.title: Prettify JSON

ui.description1: In this example, we prettify an ugly JSON array. The input data is a one-dimensional array of numbers [1,2,3] but they are all over the place. This array gets cleaned up and transformed into a more readable format where each element is on a new line with an appropriate indentation using four spaces.
ui.description2: In this example, we prettify a complex JSON data structure consisting of arrays and objects. The input data is a minified JSON object with multiple data structure depth levels. To make it neat and readable, we add two spaces for indentation to each depth level, making the JSON structure clear and easy to understand.
ui.title3: Beautify a JSON with Excessive Whitespace
ui.description3: In this example, we show how the JSON prettify tool can handle code with excessive whitespace. The input file has many leading and trailing spaces as well as spaces within the objects. The excessive whitespace makes the file bulky and hard to read and leads to a bad impression of the programmer who wrote it. The program removes all these unnecessary spaces and creates a proper data hierarchy that's easy to work with by adding indentation via tabs.

stringify.description: Convert JavaScript objects to JSON string format. Serialize data structures into JSON strings for storage or transmission.
stringify.title: Stringify JSON

ui.description1: Convert a basic JavaScript object into a JSON string.
ui.description2: Convert an array containing different types of values into JSON.
ui.description3: Convert an object to JSON with HTML characters escaped.
ui.description6: Convert HTML special characters to their entity references
ui.description7: JSON Stringify is a tool that converts JavaScript objects and arrays into their JSON string representation. It properly formats the output with customizable indentation and offers the option to escape HTML special characters, making it safe for web usage. This tool is particularly useful when you need to serialize data structures for storage or transmission, or when you need to prepare JSON data for HTML embedding.

tsvToJson.description: Convert TSV (Tab-Separated Values) data to JSON format. Transform tabular data into structured JSON objects.
tsvToJson.title: TSV to JSON

validateJson.description: Check if JSON is valid and well-formed.
validateJson.title: Validate JSON

toolInfo.description: JSON (JavaScript Object Notation) is a lightweight data-interchange format. JSON validation ensures that the structure of the data conforms to the JSON standard. A valid JSON object must have: - Property names enclosed in double quotes. - Properly balanced curly braces {}. - No trailing commas after the last key-value pair. - Proper nesting of objects and arrays. This tool checks the input JSON and provides feedback to help identify and fix common errors.
toolInfo.title: What is JSON Validation?

ui.description1: This example shows a correctly formatted JSON object. All property names and string values are enclosed in double quotes, and the overall structure is properly balanced with opening and closing braces.
ui.description2: This example demonstrates an invalid JSON object where the property names are not enclosed in double quotes. According to the JSON standard, property names must always be enclosed in double quotes. Omitting the quotes will result in a syntax error.
ui.description3: This example shows an invalid JSON object with a trailing comma after the last key-value pair. In JSON, trailing commas are not allowed because they create ambiguity when parsing the data structure.

sortJson.title: Sort JSON
sortJson.description: Sort JSON data by organizing arrays of objects based on a selected key in ascending or descending order, or alphabetically sort the keys of a JSON object.
sortJson.shortDescription: Sort JSON arrays by value or object keys alphabetically
sortJson.longDescription: This tool allows you to easily sort JSON data in a structured and predictable way. You can sort an array of objects by any key, choosing either ascending or descending order, which is useful for organizing data such as lists of users, products, or records. Alternatively, you can sort the keys of a JSON object alphabetically to improve readability and consistency. The tool supports flexible sorting modes and handles common JSON structures, making it ideal for developers, data analysts, and anyone working with JSON data who needs clean, ordered output.

options.title: Sorting Options
options.modeDescription: Choose whether to sort array items by a key's value, or sort the keys of an object alphabetically
options.keyDescription: Select the key to sort by

ui.description1: Sort a JSON array of objects alphabetically by the "name" key.
ui.description2: Sort the keys of a JSON object alphabetically in ascending order.
